property.name: kirka.port
property.description: HTTP port the Kirka Spring Boot service binds to.

property.name: kirka.install_dir
property.description: Filesystem path where kirka.jar and conf/ live.

property.name: hbase.zookeeper.quorum
property.description: Comma-separated ZooKeeper hosts for HBase.

property.name: hbase.zookeeper.property.clientPort
property.description: ZooKeeper client port.

property.name: hadoop.hdfs.uri
property.description: HDFS namenode URI used to store run artifacts.

property.name: kirka.api.naming
property.description: JSON property naming on the wire. snake_case matches the official MLflow REST API and the Python/Java/R clients. Use camel_case only for short-term compatibility with legacy consumers.

property.name: security.enabled
property.description: Master switch for authentication / authorization.

property.name: security.authentication.type
property.description: Default authentication scheme: basic, knox, kerberos.

property.name: security.users.file
property.description: Bcrypt htpasswd file consulted by Basic auth (one user per line: "user:$2a$10$..."). Mount through an Ambari-managed secret in secured clusters.

property.name: security.trusted.proxies
property.description: Comma-separated CIDRs allowed to send X-Forwarded-User. Leaving empty effectively disables the Knox header (recommended when Kirka is reachable directly). Example: 10.20.0.0/16,192.168.77.10/32.

property.name: security.admin.users
property.description: Comma-separated list of usernames promoted to ROLE_ADMIN AFTER successful authentication. ROLE_ADMIN governs /actuator/env|loggers|beans, the audit log API and the GDPR hard-delete endpoint.

property.name: security.authorization.owner.enabled
property.description: The creator of a resource always retains access, even when no Ranger policy grants it. Acts as a safety net if Ranger Admin is unreachable at startup.

property.name: security.kerberos.enabled
property.description: Enable Kerberos authentication.

property.name: security.kerberos.principal
property.description: Kerberos service principal. Ambari substitutes _HOST with the hostname where KIRKA_SERVER runs; the realm should match your KDC.

property.name: security.kerberos.keytab
property.description: Service keytab path (provisioned automatically by Ambari Kerberos when kerberos.json is honoured).

property.name: security.kerberos.krb5conf
property.description: Path to krb5.conf on the KIRKA_SERVER host.

property.name: ranger.service.name
property.description: Name of the Kirka service instance in Ranger Admin.

property.name: ranger.admin.url
property.description: Ranger Admin URL the embedded plugin pulls policies from.

property.name: ranger.policy.cache.dir
property.description: Local directory the Ranger plugin uses to cache policies between refreshes. Keep it on durable storage so the cache survives pod restarts.

property.name: server.shutdown.timeout
property.description: Timeout for Spring Boot graceful shutdown — request handlers get this window before the JVM exits. Match this with terminationGracePeriodSeconds in K8s.

property.name: multipart.max_file_size
property.description: Maximum size of a single artifact upload (multipart/form-data).

property.name: multipart.max_request_size
property.description: Maximum size of an entire multipart request, including headers.

property.name: kirka.heap.size
property.description: JVM max heap size (translates to -Xmx). 2g fits clusters with up to ~10M runs; bump to 4g+ for 100M-run workloads with wide metric history scans.

property.name: kirka.java.opts
property.description: Additional JVM options. Avoid setting -Xmx here; use kirka.heap.size.

property.name: kirka.spring.profiles.active
property.description: Spring profile the server starts with. The "prod" profile enables JSON structured logging via logback-spring.xml — recommended for log shippers.

property.name: logging.level.kirka
property.description: Default log level for the varga.kirka package.

property.name: logging.level.kirka.security
property.description: Log level for the security/auth subpackage. Bump to DEBUG when diagnosing Ranger or Basic-auth issues; do not leave at DEBUG in production.
